intent: we are not going to build the Forge platform. We are going to build the Mac Consensus Dev Agent. Utilize the local files to develop the PRDs and the README, AGENTS and CLAUDE markdown files in the github repository to build the PRD plan
scope_statement:
Build the PRD plan for the Mac Consensus Dev Agent — a macOS application that runs two LLM providers in parallel with Claude arbitration (TRD-2), orchestrates a build pipeline that generates PRDs and PR plans (TRD-3 §8.1, §9.1), coordinates multi-engineer builds via a GitHub-stored Build Ledger (TRD-4), provides a SwiftUI professional engineering UI (TRD-8), integrates GitHub for commits and CI (TRD-5), runs a self-hosted Mac CI runner for real Xcode validation (TRD-9), and enforces a security threat model for AI-agent code generation with signing credentials (TRD-11). The PRD plan will be derived from the full TRD suite (TRD-1 through TRD-12) plus the forge_architecture_context, README, AGENTS.md, and CLAUDE.md files, producing an ordered set of PRD items that the Build Pipeline can execute through its per-PRD review gate workflow.

Clarifications:
  - I just added them to the local file
  - leverage the entire local library to determine any gaps, if gaps exist then it will need to be included in the PRDs
  - you have access to all the documents already
  - you will need to select the optimal order in which to develop the app
  - leverage the entire local library to determine any gaps, if gaps exist then it will need to be included in the PRDs
  - it is available for your review

prd_plan.title: Mac Consensus Dev Agent Application Shell and Local Runtime Foundation
Build Thread, ThreadStateStore, and Checkpoint Persistence
Consensus Engine — Dual-Provider Parallel Execution with Claude Arbitration
Document Corpus Ingestion, Context Assembly, and Gap Detection
GitHub Integration for Repository Operations, Artifact Commits, and CI Coordination
Scope Decomposition and Ordered PRD Plan Generation Workflow
Per-PRD Full Document Generation and Review Gate Workflow
GitHub-Stored Build Ledger for Multi-Engineer Coordination
Security Threat Model Enforcement for AI-Agent Code Generation and Signing Credentials
Operational Observability, Cost Tracking, and Failure Recovery
Professional SwiftUI Engineering Workspace and Build Stream UI
Self-Hosted Mac CI Runner Integration and Real Xcode Validation Pipeline
End-to-End Build Pipeline Orchestration and Integration Validation

1.title: Create macOS app target and minimal SwiftUI launch shell

2.title: Add runtime path model and local directory bootstrapper

3.title: Implement service container protocols and dependency injection core

4.title: Add service lifecycle management and bootstrap orchestration

5.title: Implement structured logging facade with os_log categories and privacy defaults

6.title: Add versioned settings schema and UserDefaults-backed configuration store

7.title: Implement settings migration framework for versioned config evolution

8.title: Add error boundary primitives and non-fatal failure reporting

9.title: Implement concurrency conventions and isolated workspace allocation

10.title: Wire all foundation services into the app composition root assembly

11.title: Integrate application launch flow with bootstrap, settings init, and clean shutdown

12.title: Add startup diagnostics and environment validation for local runtime readiness

13.title: Add integration and regression test suite for application shell foundation
